LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ENOGA AUBANEL'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, ADDING PUNITIVE DAMAGE AND CONSORTIUM CLAIMS, IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES KNOWLEDGE OF THE RELEVANT FACTS (E.G., THE DUI CONVICTION) 11 MONTHS AGO (GREGG DEC. I:20-22), YET SEEKS AMENDMENT LESS THAN A MONTH BEFORE TRIAL, MAKING THE MOTION BOTH UNTIMELY AND PREJUDICIAL. (MORGAN V. SUP. CT. (1959) 172 CAL.APP.2D 527, 530.) DEFENDANTS WOULD NOT HAVE TIME FOR DISCOVERY ON THE NEW CLAIMS OR A MOTION TO STRIKE OR FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE POLICY FAVORING AMENDMENT OF PLEADINGS IS LIBERAL, BUT NOT UNBOUNDED. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: CHRISTINE L. JORDAN, CSR NO. 12262, CJORDAN@SFTC.ORG (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALEXANDRA SLOWLEY, STEWART SLOWLEY AND SHARON SLOWLEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFFS PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO MAINTAIN THEIR BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. (SEE CCP 437C(P)(2).) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REMINDED TO PROVIDE HARD COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS PURSUANT TO SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/SRB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Plaintiffs Prayer For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jan-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Bernard Thalheimer, Esq., LALEZARY LAW FIRM, LLP, (310) 550-8888, bernard@bhfirm.com, for Plaintiff ,ENOGA DE BOYOGUENO GEORGES AUBANEL
Randy A. Moss, Esq., DEMLER, ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP,(415) 949-1900, Moss@darlaw.com, for Defendants, ALEXANDRA ISABEL SLOWLEY, STEWART SLOWLEY, and SHARON SLOWLEY.
Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANTS ALEXANDRA SLOWLEY, STEWART SLOWLEY and SHARON SLOWLEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Plaintiffs Prayer For Punitive Damages.

Defendants' motion for summary adjudication of the claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendants fail to maintain their burden of production. (See CCP 437c(p)(2).)

"In the usual case, the question of whether the defendant's conduct will support an award of punitive damages is for the trier of fact [S]ummary judgment 'on the issue of punitive damages is proper' only 'when no reasonable jury could find the plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression.'" (Johnson & Johnson v. Superior Court (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 757, 762.)

"Despicable conduct has been described as conduct which is so vile, base, contemptible, miserable, wretched or loathsome that it would be looked down upon and despised by ordinary decent people. Such conduct has been described as '[having] the character of outrage frequently associated with crime." (Amer. Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal. App. 4th 1017, 1050; see also Tomaselli v. Transamerica Ins. Co. (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 1269, 1288 [describing conduct to warrant punitive damages as "evil, criminal, recklessly indifferent to the rights of the insured, or with a vexatious intention to injure."].)

Here, defendants 5-fact separate statement of undisputed facts fails to show that no reasonable trier of fact could rule in plaintiff's favor. The trier of fact could conclude that plaintiff acted despicably by engaging in the cited criminal conduct (driving while highly intoxicated, using a cellphone, speeding, illegal lane change, etc.).

Counsel for plaintiff is reminded to provide hard courtesy copies of their filings pursuant to SF Local Rule 2.7B.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/SRB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CAITLIN E. GREGG, Esq. of Lalezary Law Firm LLP (Tel: 310-550-8888) for plaintiff Enoga Aubanel;
Atty. BRIAN H. BUDDELL, Esq. of Demler, Armstrong & Rowland, LLP (Tel: 415-949-1900) for defendants Stewart Slowley and Sharon Showley.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ENOGA AUBANEL's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by the strong policy favoring liberality in amendment of pleadings. The factual allegations regarding defendant's driving while intoxicated and knowledge of the attendant risks are sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages. (Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890.) Further, the current trial date is not until September 13, 2021, which affords defendants sufficient time to conduct any additional discovery that may be required.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964 (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Continued To Sep-13-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support Thereof (Motions)
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CAITLIN E. GREGG, Esq. of Lalezary Law Firm, LLP (Tel: 310-550-8888) for Plaintiffs Enoga De Boyogueno, Georges Aubanel, and Miriam Okala;
Atty. BRIAN H. BUDDELL, Esq. of Demler, Armstrong & Rowland, LLP (Tel: 415-949-1900) for Defendants Stewart Slowley and Sharon Slowley.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ENOGA AUBANEL's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification of denying the motion WITHOUT PREJUDICE, as follows: Plaintiff's motion to file an amended complaint adding punitive damage and consortium claims is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff concedes knowledge of the relevant facts (e.g., the DUI conviction) 11 months ago (Gregg Dec. i:20-22), yet seeks amendment less than a month before trial, making the motion both untimely and prejudicial. (Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Defendants would not have time for discovery on the new claims or a motion to strike or for summary adjudication. The policy favoring amendment of pleadings is liberal, but not unbounded.

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/RBU)
